zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"於1489年2月出生在英國亨廷格多史爾時，她的父親Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote31歲，母親Lady Margaret Wolston26歲。她於1509年與Sir John Broughton在英國英格蘭貝德福爾托丁頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1559年3月14日在英國英格蘭米德萊可斯特藍德享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾陳尼斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"于1489年2月出生在英国亨廷格多史爾时，她的父亲Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote31岁，母亲Lady Margaret Wolston26岁。她于1509年与Sir John Broughton在英国英格兰貝德福爾托丁頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1559年3月14日在英国英格兰米德萊可斯特藍德享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾陳尼斯。

nameMeanings.name: "Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Grzegorz, Irena, Lucyna, Witold.

Polish (mainly Łada) and Czech (Moravian): from a short form of the Polish personal name Ładysław, Czech Ladislav, both from Vladislav (Latinized as Ladislaus), a Slavic name composed of the elements vlad ‘to rule’ (from Old Slavic vlasti, later vladati) + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’ (from slava ‘glory’). Compare 2 below.
Hungarian: from Lad(a), a short form of the personal name Ladomér, a Hungarian form of Slavic Vladimir (see Lado 2, compare 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: in
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Kyung, Sung, Chan, Duk, Eun Suk, Hee, Hyun, Hyung, Ik, Jae, Kon, Kwang Won, Chang, Chung, Chong, Hyun Jin, Inki, Jeong, Jin Tae, Joo, Myong, Ok Soon, Poon, Poong.  Southeast Asian Hung, Khen, My, Phan, Tek, Thang, Tok.

Korean: name of a family that migrated from China to Korea during the reign of Shilla's 3rd-century king Sok Yu-rye (284–298). The founding ancestor, In So, established a clan seat in the Kyodong area of Cholla North Province. Another clan seat was subsequently established by his descendent, In Pin, during the Koryo period. The surname is not common in Korea.
Cambodian: written អ៊ិន, unexplained.

variants.name: Yin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Feng, Hong, Jian, Kong, Li, Man, Min, Ning, Yuan, Bei, Chao, Ching, Chong, Chung, Heng, Yeng, Tun, Youn.  Cambodian Hai, Phal, Phon, Pu, Samnang, Tho, Thy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 殷, meaning ‘plentiful’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (殷), the name of the capital (located in present-day Henan province) of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046 BC ). Pan Geng, the 19th king of the Shang dynasty, moved the original capital Yan (located in present-day Shandong province) to Yin. Thus the Shang dynasty was also known as the Yin dynasty. After the Shang dynasty was overthrown by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ), people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from Yin Shui (殷水, ‘River Yin’), the name of a river in Shangshui in Henan Province. People who lived along the river acquired 殷 (Yin), the name of the river, as their surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 尹, a term for the official title in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Yin (尹), the name of a fief (said to have been located in Shexian in Shanxi province or Xin’an in Henan province), which was granted to Yin, the son of the legendary king Shao Hao (c. 26th century BC ). (ii) from the personal name of Shi Yin (師尹), literally meaning ‘head of officers’, the name of an official post during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ). (iii) from the second element of Ling Yin (令尹), the name of an official post in charge of the military in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). This post was held by a prince of the state of Shen called Cheng, who migrated to the state of Chu when he took up the post.

nameMeanings.name: Waiting
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: (i) variant of   Waiton  . (ii) variant of   Wighton  .

variants.name: Wighton
meanings: Scottish, English:  see  Waiton  .

variants.name: Waiton
meanings: 1 English: locative name from Market Weighton (ER Yorks), which is recorded as Wighton in the 13th century. The current pronunciation of the place-name is /wi:tən/.
2  English: locative name from Wighton (Norfolk), though some of the following bearers could belong with (1), and it is uncertain whether this surname has survived to the modern day.

nameMeanings.name: to
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Minh, Thanh, Dung, Vinh, Hung, Son, Chuong, Ha, Hien, Hoa, Huong, Mai.  Chinese Wai, Chi, Hang, Hon, Lai, Sang, Sze, Chan, Hing, Man Ching, Chen, Cheung.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 塗 (or 涂) and 屠, see   Tu   1 and 2.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 陶, see   Tao   1.

variants.name: Tu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Muoi, Anh, Dung, Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hue, Tuan, Ha, Hoa, Lien, Linh.  Chinese Chan, Chi, Hong, Qiang, Weiming, Cheng, Chu, Chuan, Hon, Jin, Liwei, Ping, Chung, Thai, Tuong, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Chang Yol, Min, Shen, Tian, Uyen.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 塗 (or 涂), meaning ‘paint, smear’ in Chinese: (i) from Tu (涂), the ancient name of the Chu river (located in present-day Anhui province and Jiangsu province). (ii) borne by descendants of Tu Qin (塗欽 or 涂欽) (246–337  AD ), an official during the Western and Eastern Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ), who migrated to southern China.（iii) shortened form of the compound surname Tu-Shan (塗山 or 涂山).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 屠, meaning ‘slaughter’ in Chinese: (i) shortened form of the compound surname Zou-Tu (鄒屠), the name of an ancient place (possibly located in Shandong province). (ii) simplified form of the placename Tu (屠阝), the name of a fief (located in Shaanxi province), which was said to be one of the families surnames, also written as Tu (荼) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (iii) from Tu Peng (屠剻), post name of officials in charge of slaughtering animals in the state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Queen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of   McQueen  .

English: from the Middle English female personal name Quene, sometimes a pet form of Quenill (Old English Cwēnhild, from cwēn ‘queen’ + hild ‘battle’). Early examples of the name are mainly from southern England, especially Sussex, but its currency in late medieval northern England has also been suggested by records from Yorkshire.
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English quene ‘woman; harlot; crone’ (Old English cwēn), but this is less likely than 2 to have given rise to a hereditary surname.

variants.name: McQueen
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuibhne ‘son of Suibhne’ a byname meaning ‘pleasant’. Compare McSweeney .

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuain, a Gaelic patronymic based on Old Norse sveinn ‘boy, servant’, which was also used as a personal name (see Swain ). This is given as the origin of the McQueen or McSwan family of Skye, although the spellings are hard to distinguish. Compare Whan .
Americanized form of French   Moquin  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique.

English: from the female personal name Elizabeth, which owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the fact that it was borne by John the Baptist's mother. The original form of the name was Hebrew Elisheva ‘my God (is my) oath’ and it appears thus in Exodus 6:23 as the name of Aaron's wife. By New Testament times the second element had been altered to Hebrew shabat ‘rest, Sabbath’. The personal name became even more popular after the canonization of Elisabeth, Duchess of Thuringia around 1230. See also Isabel .

West Indian (Dutch Caribbean): from the female personal name Elizabeth (see 1 above). It is one of the surnames (compare Martina ) that reflect the partially mother-oriented name culture of the formerly enslaved people in the West Indies.

variants.name: Behlen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Kurt.
German: metronymic from Abele or Hebele, pet forms ofthe female personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ), thesuffix -n being the (weak) genitive ending; thus ‘(husband orson) of Bele’.

variants.name: Beilke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Gerhard, Helmuth, Otto.
German:
from a pet form of Beile, itself a pet form of Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfonso, Alvarado, Augusto, Belia, Josefina, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Romero, Santa.

French, Spanish, Portuguese, and English (of French origin): from the Old French female personal name Isabel, which is first recorded in late 11th-century Paris and Maine (France). It was an alternative form of Elizabeth , Old French Elisabet, which had a shortened pet form Isabet. Isabel appears to have originated as a variant of Isabet, in which the suffix -el was substituted for -et through re-interpretation of -et as a hypocoristic diminutive. The resultant -bel was early associated with Old French bel ‘fair, beautiful’, as is evident in the French variant Isabeau. From France the name was taken to England, where it achieved considerable popularity in the late medieval period as an independent personal name alongside its rarer doublet Elizabeth. In the form Isabel it also appears in Spain and Portugal, while in Italy it took the form Isabella . The French surnames Isabel and Isabelle are particularly associated with Normandy.
History: Michel Isabel from Reux in Calvados, France, married Marie Jobidon in Château-Richer, QC, in 1673.

variants.name: Elsenpeter
meanings: German: variant of Elspeter, an extinct metronymic from a vernacular form of the personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ) or a habitational name for someone from Elsbethen near Salzburg, Austria. The surname Elsenpeter is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Elser
meanings: German: metronymic from the personal name Else, a short form of Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived near alders, from Low German else ‘alder’.
North German: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Elsen in Rhineland and Westphalia.

variants.name: Delisa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dino, Gino, Romeo.
Italian (De Lisa or D'Elisa): metronymic, with the preposition d(e) ‘of’, from the female personal names Lisa or Elisa, short forms of Elisabetta, an equivalent of Elizabeth .

variants.name: Betten
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Claus, Dieter, Manfred.
Dutch and North German: metronymic from the personal name Bette, a shortened form of Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ), or a patronymic from Bet, a pet form of Berthold (see Beth ).

variants.name: Elsner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Wolfgang, Alfons, Dieter, Egon, Ernst, Erwin, Eward, Hans, Heinz, Lutz, Manfred.
German:

northern topographic name for someone who lived near alders, from Low German else ‘alder’. It may also be a habitational name for someone from a place called with this word. In Silesia it has the same meaning, but is of ultimate Slavic origin. This surname is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovenia, where it may (also) be a translation into German of corresponding Slavic surnames.

variants.name: Elsen
meanings: German, Flemish, and Dutch: metronymic from a short form of the female personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).
German; Dutch (also Van Elsen): habitational name from a place so named in North Rhine-Westphalia.

Flemish and Dutch (also Van Elsen): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Elzen, for example in North Brabant in the Netherlands and in the provinces of Brabant and Liège in Belgium.

variants.name: Elsbernd
meanings: German: metronymic name meaning ‘Bernd, son of Els’, Els here a short form of the personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Bedford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the county seat of Bedfordshire, or a smaller place of the same name in Lancashire, or from Bedforth in Thornhill, Yorkshire. Both of the former are named with the Old English personal name Bēda + Old English ford ‘ford’. The name is now very common in Yorkshire as well as Bedfordshire.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" was born in February 1489, in Huntingdonshire, England, her father, Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote, was 31 and her mother, Lady Margaret Wolston, was 26. She married Sir John Broughton in 1509, in Toddington, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She died on 14 March 1559, in Strand, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Chenies, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" est née en février 1489, à Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Wolston, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Broughton en 1509, à Toddington, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 mars 1559, à Strand, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Chenies, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" im Februar 1489 in Huntingdonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote 31 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret Wolston 26. Sie hat 1509 in Toddington, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Sir John Broughton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 14. März 1559 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Strand, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Chenies, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" è nata il nel febbraio 1489 nel Huntingdonshire, England, sua padre, Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret Wolston, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Broughton nel 1509, nel Toddington, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 14 marzo 1559, nel Strand, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Chenies, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"が1489年2月にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Guy Abraham Sapcoteは31歳，母Lady Margaret Wolstonは26歳であった。1509年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアトッドイントンでSir John Broughtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1559年3月14日にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスストランドで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアチェニーズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I"(이)가 1489년 2월 영국 헌틴그도실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Wolston(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1509년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 토딩턴에서 Sir John Broughton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1559년 3월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스트랜드에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 천이스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" nasceu em fevereiro de 1489, em Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret Wolston, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Broughton em 1509, em Toddington, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de março de 1559, em Strand, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Chenies, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" родилась февраль 1489 г. в местности хантингдоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Wolston, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Broughton в 1509 г. в местности тоддингтон, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она умерла 14 марта 1559 г. в местности странд, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности чэниесь, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Anne Countess of Bedford Sapcote "Lady in Waiting to Queen Elizabeth I" nació en febrero de 1489, en Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Guy Abraham Sapcote, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret Wolston, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sir John Broughton en 1509, en Toddington, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 14 de marzo de 1559, en Strand, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Chenies, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
